metrics.top_terms: wv-pip, title, level, branch, branch-content, branch-id, branch-reason, alive, branch-status, status-icon, status-label, branch-answer, what, dead, death-type

sections.title: The Ontological Decision Tree
sections.text:
Exhaustive branching from Nothing to God. At every node, ALL possible answers are listed. Each dead branch shows exactly why it dies. Secular physics and Theophysics climb the same tree --- they diverge at identifiable nodes.
::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: method-box

sections.title: Methodology: Four Question Types × Four Death Conditions
sections.text:
::::::::::::::::::: method-grid
:::::: method-card
::: label

sections.title: Question Type 1
sections.text:
:::
::: title
\"Does X hold?\"
:::
::: desc
Binary with escape routes. Branches: Yes / No / Meaningless / Illusory. Max 4 branches.
:::
::::::
:::::: method-card
::: label

sections.title: Question Type 2
sections.text:
:::
::: title
\"What IS X?\"
:::
::: desc
Ontological identity. Run every philosophical category: material / mental / informational / relational / mathematical / brute / composite.
:::
::::::
:::::: method-card
::: label

sections.title: Question Type 3
sections.text:
:::
::: title
\"What grounds X?\"
:::
::: desc
Explanatory origin. Branches: nothing (brute) / external (regress risk) / itself (self-grounding) / illusory / necessary.
:::
::::::
:::::: method-card
::: label

sections.title: Question Type 4
sections.text:
:::
::: title
\"What must X be?\"
:::
::: desc
Constraint derivation. Not branching --- deduction. Given survivors, derive necessary properties.
:::
::::::
:::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::: {.method-grid style="margin-top:12px;"}
:::::: method-card
::: label

sections.title: Death Condition
sections.text:
:::
::: title

sections.title: Self-Refutation
sections.text:
:::
::: desc
The claim destroys itself when stated.
:::

:::::: method-card

sections.title: Death Condition
sections.text:

sections.text:

sections.title: Empirical Contradiction
sections.text:

Claim contradicts what we observe.

sections.title: Death Condition
sections.text:
:::
::: title

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q0-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
No, nothing exists.
:::
::: branch-reason
[SELF-REFUTATION]{.death-type .self-refute} The denial requires a denier. To say \"nothing exists\" is to instantiate a claim, which is something. The proposition destroys itself upon utterance.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q0-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Yes, something exists.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Undeniable.** Even radical skeptics (Descartes\' cogito) land here. The act of doubting is itself something. Forces Q1.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .dead}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
✕
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q0-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
The question is meaningless.
:::
::: branch-reason
[SELF-REFUTATION]{.death-type .self-refute} Declaring meaninglessness requires a meaning-bearing entity to declare it. The proposition presupposes what it denies.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Collapses
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q0-D
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Existence is an illusion.
:::
::: branch-reason
[COLLAPSES → Q0-B]{.death-type .circular} An illusion requires something to be illusory *for*. The illusion itself exists as illusion. Routes back to \"something exists.\"
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A1.1 --- \"Something exists rather than nothing.\"
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Agrees completely. No divergence.
:::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::: worldview-bar
::: wv-label

sections.title: Worldview Survival --- 18 tracked
Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q1-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
No --- being and non-being are indistinguishable.
:::
::: branch-reason
[LOGICAL INCOHERENCE]{.death-type .incoherent} Contradicts Q0-B. If existence and non-existence are indistinguishable, \"something exists\" has no content. You\'ve collapsed your own starting point.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q1-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Yes --- distinction between being and non-being is real.
:::
::: branch-reason
**This is what \"existence\" MEANS.** To exist is to be distinguishable from not-existing. Forces Q2.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Costly
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q1-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Distinction is real at one level but illusory at another.
:::
::: branch-reason
[RECURSION]{.death-type .circular} Advaita Vedanta lives here: empirical distinction is real, ultimate distinction is illusory. But stating THIS requires distinguishing the two levels --- recursion. Carries cost forward.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A1.2 --- \"Existence requires distinguishability.\"
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Agrees. Observables are defined by distinguishability.
:::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::: worldview-bar
::: wv-label

sections.title: Worldview Survival
Circular
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A property of matter --- physical objects differ.
:::
::: branch-reason
[CIRCULAR]{.death-type .circular} Presupposes matter. We haven\'t established matter yet. We only have \"something exists\" + \"distinction is real.\" What IS matter, if not a pattern of distinctions?
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Circular
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A property of mind --- observers create difference.
:::
::: branch-reason
[CIRCULAR]{.death-type .circular} Presupposes observers. We haven\'t established consciousness yet. What distinguishes the observer from non-observer?
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A distinction IS information --- the minimum unit of difference is the bit.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Definitional.** Shannon (1948) formalized exactly this: information = reduction of uncertainty between distinguishable states. One distinction = one bit. No additional primitives needed. Forces Q3.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .dead}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
✕
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-D
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A brute, unanalyzable primitive.
:::
::: branch-reason
[COLLAPSES]{.death-type .incoherent} A \"brute distinction\" carrying no informational content is indistinguishable from no distinction (violates Q1-B). If it DOES carry content, you\'ve restated Q2-C.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Merges
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-E
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A mathematical relation.
:::
::: branch-reason
[VARIANT OF Q2-C]{.death-type .circular} Mathematical relations ARE informational structures. This is Q2-C in different language. Not wrong --- not independent.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Collapses
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q2-F
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A structural relation (Ontic Structural Realism --- Ladyman/French).
:::
::: branch-reason
[GPT FIX #1]{.death-type .circular} \"Relations all the way down.\" But relations between what? If relata eliminated → relations without content → Q2-D (dead). If relata carry content → \"structure\" = \"pattern of distinctions\" → Q2-C. Not independent branch.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A1.3 --- \"Distinguishability IS information; information is ontologically primitive.\"
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Operates in Q2-A territory. Functional but logically circular at this level.
:::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::: worldview-bar
::: wv-label
First Major Kill Zone --- 6 worldviews reject A1.3 for the same structural reason
:::
::::::::::::::::::::: wv-track
::: {.wv-pip .alive title="Classical Theism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .alive title="Panentheism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Deism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Idealism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Dualism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Panpsychism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Process"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Pantheism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Materialism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Emergentism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Naturalism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Functionalism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Existentialism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Hard Determinism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Eliminativism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Nihilism — REJECTS"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .partial title="Buddhism"}
:::
::: {.wv-pip .dead-wv title="Advaita Vedanta"}
:::
:::::::::::::::::::::
::: wv-count
[2]{.alive-n} fully accept · [9]{.partial-n} partial · [7]{.dead-n} reject
:::
::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.level level="3"}
:::::: {.level-header onclick="toggleLevel(this)"}
[▼]{.expand-hint}
::: level-tag
Level 3 --- Order [Type 1: Does X hold?]{.qtype}
:::
::: level-question
Q3: Does information require structure, or can it exist as pure randomness?
:::
::: level-context
Forced by Q2-C. Pure noise = maximum Shannon entropy = zero usable content. Can randomness ground reality?
:::
::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: branches
:::::::::: {.branch .dead}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
✕
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q3-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Information can be purely random and still ground reality.
:::
::: branch-reason
[EMPIRICAL CONTRADICTION]{.death-type .empirical} Random information carries no structure. A reality grounded in pure noise has no laws, no regularities, no physics. Contradicts observed reality.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q3-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Information must have structure --- pattern, regularity, compression.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Both systems agree.** Reality has structure. The question is what EXPLAINS it. Forces Q4.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Deferred
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q3-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Structure is observer-imposed, not inherent.
:::
::: branch-reason
[DEFERRED]{.death-type .circular} Presupposes observers again. And for the information itself, it\'s random until observed → Q3-A. Kicks the can.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A3.1 --- Order is required. Coherence is built into substrate.
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Agrees structure exists. Takes it as given --- \"the laws just are.\"
:::
:::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.level level="4"}
:::::: {.level-header onclick="toggleLevel(this)"}
[▼]{.expand-hint}
::: level-tag
Level 4 --- The Great Fork [Type 3: What grounds X?]{.qtype}
:::
::: level-question
Q4: What grounds the structure?
:::
::: level-context
THE primary divergence point. Secular physics and Theophysics take different branches here. Both are logically valid --- but one terminates and one continues.
:::
::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: branches
:::::::::: {.branch .terminal}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
■
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Terminal
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q4-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Nothing --- the substrate is a brute fact.
:::
::: branch-reason
[EXPLANATORY TERMINAL]{.death-type .explanatory} **This is where secular physics lives. Honestly.** \"The laws are what they are.\" Logically coherent. Epistemically closed. No further questions answerable. Not a contradiction --- a boundary. (GPT Fix #2: upgraded from \"dead\" to \"terminal.\")
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .dead}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
✕
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q4-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Another structure grounds it --- turtles all the way down.
:::
::: branch-reason
[INFINITE REGRESS]{.death-type .regress} Every \"ground\" requires a deeper ground. No explanation is ever complete. No foundation ever reached.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q4-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
The substrate grounds itself --- self-consistent and self-referencing.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Self-grounding.** The \"why\" and the \"what\" are identical at the deepest level. The system couldn\'t NOT exist --- mathematical necessity. Forces Q5 (property derivation).
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Weak
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q4-D
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
A multiverse generates all possible substrates --- anthropic selection.
:::
::: branch-reason
[UNFALSIFIABLE + REGRESS]{.death-type .explanatory} (a) No observable test distinguishes this from Q4-A. (b) What grounds the multiverse mechanism? Pushes question back. (c) Violates parsimony.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Collapses
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q4-E
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
The question is illegitimate --- \"why\" has no answer.
:::
::: branch-reason
[COLLAPSES → Q4-A]{.death-type .circular} Saying \"no answer exists\" IS accepting brute fact. Same position, philosophical hat.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A2.2 --- Self-grounding substrate → D2.1 Logos Field χ(x,t)
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Q4-A. Honest stopping point. Physics describes; it doesn\'t explain ground.
:::
:::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::: worldview-bar
::: wv-label

sections.title: The Great Fork --- Tree splits into two families
DERIVED PROPERTIES
sections.text:
:::
::: {.branch-reason style="line-height:2;"}
**Self-consistent** --- A self-contradictory ground destroys itself. (A2.2)\
**Contains its own explanation** --- Incomplete self-reference can\'t close the loop. Falls to Q4-B.\
**Non-material** --- Specific material config = contingent, not necessary. (G1.3)\
**Non-temporal** --- If ground depends on time, time needs grounding → regress. (G1.2)\
**Maximally coherent** --- Incoherent self-grounding fragments and destroys itself. (A3.1, D3.1)\
**Non-deceptive** --- Deception = maintaining P and ¬P toward subsystems = internal incoherence = coherence failure. Follows from maximal coherence, not self-consistency alone. (G2.4) *(GPT Fix #3)*\
**Generative** --- Must produce the world we observe. Inert ground explains nothing. (D3.3, E3.1)
:::
:::::
::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: Properties match --- universal, eternal, immaterial, coherent, non-deceptive, generative.
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Cannot derive these. Stopped at Q4-A. No ground to derive from.
:::
:::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.level level="6"}
:::::: {.level-header onclick="toggleLevel(this)"}
[▼]{.expand-hint}
::: level-tag
Level 6 --- Observer Requirement [Type 1: Does X hold?]{.qtype}
:::
::: level-question
Q6: Does the ground require actualization --- does information become actual on its own?
:::
::: level-context
The measurement problem. Potential vs actual. Superposition persists until observation --- why?
:::
::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: branches
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Problematic
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q6-A
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Information self-actualizes --- no observer needed.
:::
::: branch-reason
[CONTRADICTS QM]{.death-type .empirical} If information self-actualizes, why does superposition exist at all? The measurement problem IS the question of how potential becomes actual.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .continues}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
→
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Continues
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q6-B
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Information requires observation to become actual.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Participatory universe.** Wheeler\'s delayed-choice. The Born rule. Potential requires actualization, actualization requires observer. Forces Q7.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .problematic}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
⚠
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Incomplete
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q6-C
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
Decoherence handles it --- spontaneous collapse.
:::
::: branch-reason
[INCOMPLETE]{.death-type .circular} Explains appearance of collapse, not the thing itself. Wigner\'s Friend: where does the observer chain end?
:::
::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.branch .dead}
::::: branch-status
::: status-icon
✕
:::
::: status-label

sections.title: Dead End
sections.text:
:::
:::::
:::::: branch-content
::: branch-id

sections.title: Q6-D
sections.text:
:::
::: branch-answer
No potential/actual distinction --- everything always actual.
:::
::: branch-reason
[EMPIRICAL CONTRADICTION]{.death-type .empirical} Superposition is experimentally verified. Interference patterns require it.
:::
::::::
::::::::::
::::: system-map
::: {.sys-badge .theophysics}
SYS-B: A5.1 --- Observer required for actualization. A5.2 --- Observers participate.
:::
::: {.sys-badge .secular}
SYS-A: Split --- Many-Worlds (Q6-A), Copenhagen (Q6-B), Decoherence (Q6-C). No consensus.
:::
:::::
::::::::::::::::: modes-sidebar
::: modes-label
⚡ Four Modes Activate --- first finite agent enters the tree
:::

sections.text:

Finite bandwidth --- can\'t observe everything. Structural blind spots.

sections.text:

sections.title: Manipulable perception --- adversarial agents can distort inputs
sections.text:

:::::
::::: {.mode-item .m4}
::: mode-name

sections.title: M4: Grace Attenuation
sections.text:
:::
::: mode-val
Clarity depends on coupling to coherence field. Low coupling = distortion.
:::

::: branch-answer
Yes --- the chain terminates in a TERMINAL OBSERVER.
:::
::: branch-reason
**Derived properties:** Always actual (never in superposition). Self-grounding. Infinite observational capacity (Φ = ∞). Non-material. **All four deviation modes = 0.** Forces Q8.

The chain loops --- observers observe each other circularly.

::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.level level="8"}

[▼]{.expand-hint}
::: level-tag
Level 8 --- Boundary Conditions [Type 4: What must X be?]{.qtype}
:::
::: level-question
Q8: What boundary conditions must a complete theory satisfy?
:::
::: level-context
8 necessary conditions derived from the accumulated constraints of Q0--Q7. Not assumptions --- consequences.
:::
::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::: branches
:::::: {.branch .continues style="flex-direction:column;"}
::::: branch-content
::: branch-id
8 BOUNDARY CONDITIONS
:::
::: {.branch-reason style="line-height:2.2;"}
**BC1** Terminal Observer exists (Φ = ∞) --- from Q7-B\
**BC2** Grace external to system --- coherence can\'t self-increase (T3.1)\
**BC3** Measurement orthogonal to observable --- observer independent of observed\
**BC4** N~observers~ = 3 for zero-uncertainty closure --- *formally pending (honest blank)*\
**BC5** Superposition preserved until collapse --- free will requires genuine potentiality\
**BC6** Infinite energy source for entropy defeat --- Second Law requirement\
**BC7** Information conserved through all transformations --- unitarity\
**BC8** Coupling must be voluntary --- agent freely accepts or rejects grace
:::

::::::

::: modes-label

sections.title: IDENTIFICATION MAPPING
sections.text:
:::
::: {.branch-reason style="line-height:2.2;"}
BC1 (Φ = ∞ observer) → **Omniscience**\
BC2 (External grace) → **Grace theology** (Eph 2:8-9)\
BC3 (Orthogonal measurement) → **Divine transcendence**\
BC4 (N = 3 observers) → **Trinity** (Father, Son, Spirit)\
BC5 (Superposition until collapse) → **Free will** (Deut 30:19)\
BC6 (Infinite energy source) → **Omnipotence**\
BC7 (Information conservation) → **Immortal soul, resurrection**\
BC8 (Voluntary coupling) → **\"Whoever wills\"** (Rev 22:17)
:::
:::::

Q10: Can a system change its own moral orientation?

Given sign structure σ = ±1 --- can an agent flip its own sign? This is where grace becomes structurally necessary.

::: branch-answer
Yes --- self-improvement can change orientation.
:::
::: branch-reason
[CONTRADICTS T3.1]{.death-type .incoherent} Coherence cannot self-increase in closed system. Self-generated operations commute with sign operator: \[σ̂, Û\] = 0.

:::

**The system cannot flip itself.** An external, non-unitary operator (Ĝ) is required. This IS the grace requirement --- C8.2: works salvation impossible.
:::

**Atheism --- 0/8**: BC1 ✗ BC2 ✗ BC3 ✗ BC4 ✗ BC5 ✗ BC6 ✗ BC7 ✗ BC8 ⚠\

sections.title: The Honest Blanks
Secular Physics Blanks
sections.text:
::: blank-item
Why does anything exist? --- No answer. Brute fact.
:::
::: blank-item
Why these laws? --- No answer. \"Just are.\"
:::
::: blank-item
What IS consciousness? --- 5 competing interpretations, none complete.
:::
::: blank-item
Why does superposition collapse? --- No consensus.
:::
::: blank-item
Why does the universe appear designed? --- Anthropic principle ≠ explanation.
:::
::: blank-item
What grounds mathematics? --- Open question.
:::
::: blank-item
Why moral intuition? --- Evolution explains existence, not truth.
:::
::::::::::
:::::::::: {.blanks-col .theo-blanks}

equations: Forced by Q2-C. Pure noise = maximum Shannon entropy = zero usable content. Can randomness ground reality?
**Non-material** --- Specific material config = contingent, not necessary. (G1.3)\
**Non-deceptive** --- Deception = maintaining P and ¬P toward subsystems = internal incoherence = coherence failure. Follows from maximal coherence, not self-consistency alone. (G2.4) *(GPT Fix #3)*\
Terminal Observer: All Four Modes = Zero
**BC4** N~observers~ = 3 for zero-uncertainty closure --- *formally pending (honest blank)*\
